tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, ketones, glucosuria), suggesting possible renal dysfunction or early nephropathy. HbA1c is low, which may indicate recent hypoglycemia or altered red cell turnover. No evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical context, including diabetes status and possible liver involvement.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
